title: Imaging the source region of recent megathrust earthquakes along the Chile subduction zone: A summary of results from recent experiments
journal: Journal of South American Earth Sciences
abstract: The paper examines the largest documented earthquake, the Mw 9.5 Valdivia earthquake of 1960, and three subsequent major earthquakes occurring along the Chilean margin. The authors present findings from marine seismological surveys designed to understand how geological structures influenced rupture patterns, with emphasis on sediment distribution and basement topography characteristics. Results from multiple recent experiments including MGL1610 (PICTURES, 2016), the 2017 south-central Chile survey (MGL1701, CEVICHE), and TIPTEQ-related data are synthesized. The review covers marine seismic imaging of source regions of the 1960, 2010, 2014, and 2015 Chilean megathrust earthquakes.

location.description: Full Chilean subduction zone from ~19°S to ~46°S, synthesis of multiple marine seismic experiments targeting megathrust earthquake source regions

data.notes: MCS reflection shot data (SEGD format) from the PICTURES experiment (MGL1610, 2016) offshore northern Chile (19°–21°S). Multiple trench-perpendicular profiles targeting the Iquique earthquake source region.
51 OBS stations (XW network, 2016) deployed during MGL1610 cruise offshore northern Chile. Wide-angle seismic refraction data accessible via IRIS/EarthScope web services.
Raw multibeam swath bathymetry, backscatter, and sidescan data (2,642 files, ~170 GB) acquired with Kongsberg EM122 during the 2016 PICTURES (MGL1610) cruise offshore northern Chile 19°–21°S.
Raw underway gravimeter data (Bell BGM-3, 48 files, ~167 MB) from the 2016 PICTURES (MGL1610) cruise. A processed gravity product (~696 MB) is also available via RVData.
Raw magnetometer data (Geometrics G-882, 65 files, ~2.6 GB) from the 2016 PICTURES (MGL1610) cruise. A processed magnetics product (~2.5 GB) is also available.
Primary navigation (GPS positions) data from the 2016 PICTURES (MGL1610) cruise, C-Nav 3050 system, archived via Rolling Deck to Repository (R2R).
Processed seismic shot time navigation data from the MGL1610 PICTURES cruise, offshore Chile. For use when processing the MCS reflection data.
Raw singlebeam/subbottom profiler data (Knudsen 3260, 50 files, ~1.1 GB) from the 2016 PICTURES (MGL1610) cruise.

MCS reflection shot data (SEGD) from the CEVICHE experiment (MGL1701, Jan–Feb 2017) along ~1000 km of south-central Chile margin (30°–44°S). 1212-channel, 15.1-km streamer, targeting 2015 Illapel, 2010 Maule, and 1960 Valdivia earthquake source regions. Cruise DOI: 10.7284/907175.
Raw multibeam swath bathymetry data (1,753 files, ~201 GB) from the 2017 CEVICHE (MGL1701) cruise, offshore south-central Chile 30°–44°S.
Raw underway gravimeter data from the 2017 CEVICHE (MGL1701) cruise, offshore south-central Chile.
Raw magnetometer data from the 2017 CEVICHE (MGL1701) cruise, offshore south-central Chile.
Primary navigation (GPS positions) data from the 2017 CEVICHE (MGL1701) cruise, archived via R2R.

processing.summary: Review and synthesis paper summarizing results from multiple recent marine seismic experiments along the Chilean subduction zone: MGL1610 PICTURES (2016) and MGL1701 CEVICHE (2017) south-central Chile survey. Data from both experiments fully processed to migrated depth sections. Results compared across the four major recent Chilean megathrust earthquakes (1960, 2010, 2014, 2015). Emphasis on sediment distribution and basement topography as controls on rupture.
workflow: 1. Compilation of results from PICTURES (MGL1610, 2016) experiment
2. Compilation of results from CEVICHE (MGL1701, 2017) south-central Chile survey
3. Integration with TIPTEQ and prior GEOMAR data
4. Synthesis of structural controls on rupture for 1960, 2010, 2014, 2015 events
processing.notes: Review paper synthesizing multiple experiments. Tréhu (OSU) and Bangs (UTIG) principal investigators for PICTURES and CEVICHE surveys. No new data acquired for this paper.

analysis_notes: Paper behind paywall; no open access version found. Abstract reconstructed from survey snippet. Review paper synthesizing PICTURES (MGL1610, 2016) and CEVICHE (MGL1701, 2017) datasets. The 2017 cruise is confirmed as MGL1701 (CEVICHE project, Jan–Feb 2017, Bangs/Trehu PIs, cruise DOI 10.7284/907175). All MGL1610 and MGL1701 data confirmed open access at MGDS/RVData.
